negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: Missing accepted commit-block lookback bounds for fulfilled settlement building; the port reads live contract state and checks absent/settled/expired/mismatch cases, but no lookback-bound policy was found.
related_but_insufficient_code: prepare_fulfilled_async_settlement reads AsyncJobTracker state and skips when job is absent, phase1-settled, expired, or pool/contract metadata mismatches.
Verifier uses the same live job state for settlement checks and expiry, with delivery-side current_block >= commit_block checks.

rationale: The current contract-state settlement invariant supersedes stale pool-metadata dependence and is further constrained by bounded neighbor context around active/expired job semantics and commit_block removal.
statement: Before building a fulfilled async settlement, payload building must read the job from AsyncJobTracker contract state and skip settlement when the job is absent, already phase-1 settled, expired by contract expiry, outside accepted commit-block lookback bounds, or otherwise disagrees with verifier timing boundaries.
suggested_falsification_tests: Create a fulfilled async job with commit_block older than the accepted lookback and assert payload building skips it.
Parity test builder/verifier settlement timing boundaries for absent, settled, expired, too-old, and future-commit jobs.
summary: Settlement building uses live contract state, but commit-block lookback bounds are missing.
test_or_falsification_candidate: A too-old commit_block fixture should be skipped by payload building; no local lookback-bound implementation was found.
title: Settlement building uses contract-live async job state
